title: Make the wgpu backend a production-quality library backend
description: Operator direction: use the cacophony project as inspiration for canonical wgpu setup examples, but do not cargo-cult it because GPU support there may not have worked reliably. Treat kittui as a well-written library: design a robust wgpu backend abstraction with explicit adapter/device lifecycle, headless/offscreen compatibility on macOS/Linux, deterministic CPU fallback, clear errors, resource reuse across frames, no hidden global state, parity tests against CPU for supported nodes, and documentation of unsupported GPU features. This should refine/coordinate with bd-6c1165 (probe cache) and the renderer semantic beads rather than leaving GPU as a best-effort stub.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add fuzz targets and workspace integration smoke from DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md calls for cargo-fuzz targets for Scene::from_json and CLI flag parsers plus a workspace integration smoke test that builds a runtime, renders a small scene, verifies PNG bytes, and checks placement escapes. Current repo has unit/golden/parity/ABI tests, but no fuzz/ directory and no root tests/it.rs smoke. Add bounded fuzz targets and a fast integration smoke that can run in CI without a real kitty terminal.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: add fuzz targets and integration tests

title: Add nix flake
description: Add a nix flake to the project. See tendril and other rust caco projects as examples for implementation details and best practices.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: Implement deterministic Scene normalization before hashing
description: DESIGN.md says Scene::id() must hash a deterministic normalized scene: drop empty/zero-opacity layers, flatten normal composites, snap pixel rects to 1/64 px, clamp/sort gradient stops, and remove zero-width/transparent structural no-ops. Current crates/kittui-core/src/scene.rs hashes the raw serde form directly, so semantically equivalent scenes can miss the cache and produce different kitty ids. Add a normalizer in kittui-core, test id stability/equivalence with property or fixture tests, and keep serde JSON compatibility stable.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add renderer probe cache and GPU parity gating to Runtime Auto mode
description: DESIGN.md says Runtime Auto should try GPU once, run a CPU/GPU parity fixture with SSIM tolerance, persist gpu_status/gpu_adapter/gpu_parity_ssim in <cache>/probe.json, and skip future live probes until invalidated. kittui-cache has a ProbeRecord helper, but Runtime currently only lazily initializes GPU and permanently falls back within the process without parity checking or persisted probe decisions. Wire probe.json into RuntimeBuilder/Auto mode, implement probe --force invalidation, and test fallback behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete terminal transport probing and File/Memory transfer modes
description: DESIGN.md specifies TerminalInfo::detect(), automatic tmux detection, and kitty File/Memory transports (`a=t,t=f` and `a=t,t=s`) in addition to Direct/TmuxPassthrough. Current TerminalInfo only provides defaults/overrides, and kittui-kitty treats File/Memory like direct chunked base64. Implement environment probing over TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/KITTY_WINDOW_ID/KITTY_PUBLIC_KEY/TMUX/WT_SESSION, CLI transport override, file-transfer emission, shared-memory transfer where supported with safe fallback, and tests for all transport encodings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement real Mask, Clip, and BlendMode rendering semantics
description: DESIGN.md includes Node::Composite with BlendMode::{Normal, Add, Multiply, Screen}, Node::Mask, and Node::Clip. Current CPU rasterizer passes through Composite children using normal source-over and renders Mask/Clip children without applying mask alpha or clip bounds; GPU encode similarly ignores the blend/mask/clip semantics. Implement matching CPU/GPU semantics or explicit CPU-first semantics with golden tests for add/multiply/screen, masked content, and clipped content.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement user Shader node rendering and pipeline cache
description: DESIGN.md/Future ideas mention Node::Shader with per-scene WGSL pipeline compilation, caching, and GPU execution, while CPU should return a clear GPU-only error. Current GPU encode accepts Shader in the type system but draws nothing, and CPU returns UnsupportedImage. Implement shader validation, uniform binding, per-scene pipeline cache, error reporting, and tests that Shader nodes either render through GPU or fail clearly under CPU-only mode.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Finish v1 CLI surface from DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md lists a broader CLI than currently implemented: panel, image, place, scene emit, cache info/gc/clear, probe --force, --transport, --renderer, --upload-only/--placement-only/--embed-only, --dry-run, pixel and percentage sizing, richer colors, glow/scanline/shadow/label options, and animation curve validation. Some cache commands exist, but most common flags and subcommands do not. Fill out the v1 CLI surface in thin wrappers over Runtime/Composition and add integration tests for JSON/dry-run/raw output modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add layered kittui configuration resolution
description: Operator direction: every user-facing kittui option should be settable via explicit CLI/API flag, KITTUI_* environment variable, or ~/.config/kittui/config.yaml, with precedence flag/API override > env override > YAML default. This lets scripts establish an env/config scope and call kittui many times or batch operations without repeating every flag, while apps can still force overrides. Design and implement a typed Config model, YAML loader (respecting XDG_CONFIG_HOME), KITTUI_* env parsing, source/precedence diagnostics for --json output, validation/error messages, and tests covering precedence and malformed config.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement Image node rasterization and kittui image CLI
description: DESIGN.md defines Node::Image plus CLI `kittui image --src PATH -w W -h H --fit ... --tint ...`. Current CPU renderer returns UnsupportedImage for Node::Image and GPU encode drops image nodes; the CLI has no image subcommand. Implement PNG/JPEG image decoding behind the intended feature split, shared Fit geometry, CPU raster copy/tint path, GPU texture/atlas or documented CPU fallback path, image cache storage, and CLI coverage with tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Surface project config validation blockers in caco commands
description: While finishing bd-5c2b47 in the kittui project, every caco surface I tried (`caco msg speak`, `caco scratch append`, and `caco agent reintegrate`) failed because an unrelated `projects['picasso'].friend_projects[0].permissions` entry contained invalid permission `checkout_write`. The operator fixed the config, but the failure mode was surprising because a picasso config issue blocked kittui lifecycle/status narration and scratch health-note reconciliation. Consider making config validation errors identify the owning config path/project and, if safe, isolate unrelated project operations from invalid friend-project permission entries. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-5c2b47 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete FFI ABI surface and generated header workflow
description: DESIGN.md defines a larger C ABI: runtime_configure, render_json, unplace, last_error, probe_json, bytes_free, abi_version_check, explicit byte lengths, cbindgen header snapshots, and ABI drift checks. Current kittui-ffi exposes runtime_new/free, place_json, string_free, abi_version, and a hand-maintained header/test scaffold. Implement the remaining ABI functions, preserve panic-safe boundaries, generate/update kittui.h through an xtask/cbindgen workflow, and add ABI snapshot validation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: complete ffi abi surface and header workflow

title: Add batch/many render entrypoints that share resolved config and runtime state
description: Operator direction: common usage should support setting config/env once, then calling kittui many times or in a batch in that scope. Add well-documented batch entrypoints that resolve config once, reuse Runtime/cache/probe state, render/place many scenes efficiently, and expose this through CLI (e.g. JSONL or manifest input), Rust library, and FFI/TS binding surfaces. Ensure per-item overrides remain possible and tests verify repeated calls do not re-probe/re-upload unnecessarily.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Expand kittui-wm from placeholder substrate to split/stack/tab layout semantics
description: DESIGN.md says kittui-wm currently reserves WindowTree/WindowGeometry but full split/stack/tab semantics and per-window scene generation should arrive after tmux showcase exercises diff-driven composition. Current crate description/source mark kittui-wm as a placeholder scaffold. Implement basic split, stack, and tab layout models, z-order handling, conversion to Composition entries, and tests for deterministic geometry output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

bead.title: Add layered kittui configuration resolution
bead.description: Operator direction: every user-facing kittui option should be settable via explicit CLI/API flag, KITTUI_* environment variable, or ~/.config/kittui/config.yaml, with precedence flag/API override > env override > YAML default. This lets scripts establish an env/config scope and call kittui many times or batch operations without repeating every flag, while apps can still force overrides. Design and implement a typed Config model, YAML loader (respecting XDG_CONFIG_HOME), KITTUI_* env parsing, source/precedence diagnostics for --json output, validation/error messages, and tests covering precedence and malformed config.
bead.creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

bead.title: Fix macOS kittui-cli link failure for missing libiconv
bead.description: While validating bd-c3214b on ms-mac, cargo build -p kittui-cli and cargo test -p kittui-cli --bins compiled successfully but failed at link time with ld: library not found for -liconv. cargo check -p kittui-cli passes, so this appears to be host/toolchain linker configuration or an existing native dependency issue rather than a source compile error from the config change. Command evidence: cargo build -p kittui-cli -> cc ... -liconv ... ld: library not found for -liconv. Filed from bd-c3214b validation.
bead.creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

bead.title: Fix macOS kittui-cli link failure for missing libiconv

Command evidence: cargo build -p kittui-cli -> cc ... -liconv ... ld: library not found for -liconv. Filed from bd-c3214b validation.
bead.creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
